findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer depends on full QueryExecutor rather than a narrow port, over-exposing the exploration layer
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer constructor takes QueryExecutor and passes it to MultiSeedExplorer, SingleSeedExplorer, and CollocateProfileComparator
MultiSeedExplorer only uses executor.executeSurfacePattern (one method from SketchQueryPort); SingleSeedExplorer uses executeSurfacePattern and executeCollocations (two methods from different ports)
CollocateProfileComparator also only needs executeCollocations, which is in CollocateQueryPort
findings.suggestion: Introduce a narrow CollocateAndSketchPort (or reuse the existing port hierarchy) that covers the two methods actually needed by the exploration layer. Declare SemanticFieldExplorer constructor parameter as that narrower type. This aligns with the established narrow-port pattern already used by SketchHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, and CorpusQueryHandlers.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer's public constructor accepts @Nullable GrammarConfig with a Javadoc that says production callers must supply non-null
evidence: Constructor signature: public SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor, CollocateProfileComparator comparator, MultiSeedExplorer multiSeedExplorer, @Nullable GrammarConfig grammarConfig)
Javadoc says: 'null is accepted as a test-only escape hatch... Production callers must always supply a non-null, fully-loaded grammar config'
The @Nullable annotation contradicts the documented production contract; any caller passing null is not caught at compile time by null-safety tooling
findings.suggestion: Remove @Nullable from the grammarConfig parameter and enforce non-null with Objects.requireNonNull. Move the null-accepting test path into a dedicated package-private test-only factory method or test subclass, keeping the public contract clean. If the escape hatch is truly needed in tests, annotate with a test-only marker and document separately.

findings.summary: WordSketchApiServer stores grammarConfig as a private final field but never reads it after construction
evidence: Line 47: private final GrammarConfig grammarConfig;
Line 62: this.grammarConfig = Objects.requireNonNull(grammarConfig, ...);
grammarConfig is used within the constructor body (passed to handlers, used in validateAliases) but the field is never read in start(), stop(), registerRoutes(), getPort(), or any other method
findings.suggestion: Remove the grammarConfig field. The grammarConfig local parameter can still be passed directly to the handler constructors and validateAliases without being stored. If future methods genuinely need it, re-add the field with a comment explaining why.

findings.summary: Sibling exploration endpoints expose edge weight as 'log_dice' (explore/explore-multi) vs 'weight' (compare) in JSON responses
evidence: ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry: record EdgeEntry(String source, String target, @JsonProperty("log_dice") double logDice, RelationEdgeType type) — JSON key is 'log_dice'
ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry: record EdgeEntry(String source, String target, double weight, RelationEdgeType type) — JSON key is 'weight' (no @JsonProperty rename)
Both types represent the same logDice association score between two graph nodes, used in semantically related sibling endpoints (/explore, /explore-multi, /compare)
findings.suggestion: Unify the two EdgeEntry types: replace ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry with ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry (or a shared type in api.model) and apply @JsonProperty("log_dice") consistently. Update ExploreResponseAssembler.buildComparisonResponse to build ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry instances instead of ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry. This also eliminates the duplicate type definition.

findings.summary: ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry duplicates ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry structure — same four fields, separate type definitions
evidence: ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry: (String source, String target, double weight, RelationEdgeType type)
ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry: (String source, String target, @JsonProperty("log_dice") double logDice, RelationEdgeType type)
ExploreResponseAssembler.buildComparisonResponse maps Edge→ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry; buildEdgeEntries maps Edge→ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry — two separate mapping paths for the same model object
findings.suggestion: Remove ComparisonResponse.EdgeEntry. Reuse ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry (or extract a shared EdgeEntry to api.model package). Update ComparisonResponse to hold List<ExploreResponse.EdgeEntry> (or List<shared.EdgeEntry>) for the edges field. This removes one mapping path in ExploreResponseAssembler and aligns the JSON shape.

findings.summary: CORS allow-origin is read from System.getProperty on every HTTP response rather than captured once at server start
evidence: setCorsHeader(HttpExchange exchange) calls System.getProperty("cors.allow.origin", DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN) on every response — every call to sendJsonResponse, sendError, sendOptionsResponse, etc.
warnIfWildcardCors() also reads the property at start() time, so the validation snapshot and the runtime snapshot can diverge if the property is mutated between start() and request handling
findings.suggestion: Capture the CORS origin once in warnIfWildcardCors() and return it (or store it in a package-private field on HttpApiUtils initialised at server start). Pass the resolved origin string to setCorsHeader. This eliminates the per-request global mutable read while preserving the test-override capability via System.setProperty before server start.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer class Javadoc claims 'Thin facade' but the class owns non-trivial logic in fetchExamples
evidence:
Line 46: '*

Thin facade wiring {@link SingleSeedExplorer}, {@link MultiSeedExplorer}, and' — but fetchExamples (lines 158–194) implements a deduplication loop, BCQL pattern construction, buffer management, and sentence filtering that are non-trivial and not delegated to any sub-component
findings.suggestion: Replace 'Thin facade wiring ...' with accurate language such as 'Coordinator class wiring ...' or 'Public entry point that delegates algorithmic work to ... and owns the fetchExamples deduplication logic.' The existing fetchExamples method body is the clearest evidence that the facade claim is wrong.

findings.summary: DiscoveredNoun model class is reused in multi-seed mode to represent input seed words, inverting its stated semantic role
evidence: DiscoveredNoun Javadoc: 'A noun discovered during exploration — shares collocates with the seed'

MultiSeedExplorer.buildDiscoveredNouns (line 115) iterates over `seeds` and constructs a DiscoveredNoun for each seed (e.g. 'theory', 'model') — these are the input seed words, not nouns discovered by reverse expansion
The JSON serializer compensates by using 'source_seeds' as the key in MultiSeed responses, but the model type name still says 'DiscoveredNoun'
findings.suggestion: Introduce a second model type (e.g. `SeedProfile`) that represents per-seed collocate statistics — shared collocates, counts, scores — and use it in the multi-seed path. DiscoveredNoun should remain specific to single-seed reverse-expansion results. ExplorationResult could carry `List<DiscoveredNoun> discoveredNouns` (single-seed) and `List<SeedProfile> seedProfiles` (multi-seed) as distinct fields, removing the dual-use ambiguity.

findings.summary: SingleSeedExplorationOptions adds three trivial delegation methods that duplicate ExplorationOptions accessors without added policy
evidence: topCollocates(), logDiceThreshold(), minShared() each contain a single-line return delegating to base(); no validation, transformation, or policy is added
Callers that already have the base() object (e.g. ExplorationHandlers, which constructs both) could pass it directly; the delegation only saves one .base() call at each downstream call site
findings.suggestion: Remove the three delegation methods from SingleSeedExplorationOptions and let callers use opts.base().topCollocates() etc., or flatten the record to include all four fields directly (topCollocates, logDiceThreshold, minShared, reverseExpansionLimit) without the nested base() component. Either approach eliminates the parallel accessor surface.

findings.summary: fetchExamples fetches exactly maxExamples hits then deduplicates by sentence, silently returning fewer examples when hits share sentences
evidence: Line 171: `results = executor.executeBcqlQuery(bcqlQuery, maxExamples)` — fetches at most maxExamples CollocateResult objects

Lines 176–185: deduplication loop collects unique sentences up to maxExamples, then breaks — but if the source list is exhausted before reaching maxExamples unique sentences (due to duplicate sentence strings across multiple hits), the result count is silently less than maxExamples
The `if (deduped.size() == maxExamples) break` guard on line 183 can never fire in the under-supply case because the outer for-loop terminates first
findings.suggestion: Fetch a larger buffer before deduplicating: replace `executor.executeBcqlQuery(bcqlQuery, maxExamples)` with `executor.executeBcqlQuery(bcqlQuery, maxExamples * 4)` (or a configurable over-fetch multiplier), and keep the dedup loop unchanged. This ensures the deduplication step has enough candidates to fill maxExamples unique sentences in the common case.

findings.summary: Public SemanticFieldExplorer constructor accepts @Nullable GrammarConfig as test escape hatch, weakening production type contract
evidence: Constructor parameter annotated @Nullable GrammarConfig grammarConfig with Javadoc: 'null is accepted as a test-only escape hatch'
ExplorationHandlers enforces @NonNull GrammarConfig on its own constructor, creating inconsistent null semantics at adjacent seams
Tests invoke new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor, null) to bypass grammar config — exploits the public API weakening
findings.suggestion: Introduce a package-private or test-only factory (e.g. SemanticFieldExplorer.forTesting(executor)) rather than weakening the public constructor annotation. Keep the public constructor @NonNull.

findings.summary: ExplorationHandlers enforces @NonNull GrammarConfig but SemanticFieldExplorer's public constructor allows @Nullable, creating inconsistent seam contracts
evidence: ExplorationHandlers constructor: Objects.requireNonNull(grammarConfig, ...) — actively rejects null
SemanticFieldExplorer public constructor: @Nullable GrammarConfig grammarConfig — actively accepts null
Adjacent layers enforce opposite rules on the same value; a developer reading one seam gets misleading information about the other
findings.suggestion: Align null semantics: make SemanticFieldExplorer's public constructor @NonNull and introduce a package-private test constructor or factory for null-grammar use cases.

findings.summary: buildDiscoveredNouns uses DoubleSummaryStatistics but only needs avg and sum; min/max/count computed and discarded
evidence: java.util.DoubleSummaryStatistics stats = sharedCollocates.values().stream().mapToDouble(Double::doubleValue).summaryStatistics()
Only stats.getAverage() and stats.getSum() are used; getMin(), getMax(), getCount() are never read
Could be replaced with two direct stream reductions or a simple loop accumulating sum and count
findings.suggestion: Replace with explicit sum/count accumulation: double sum = sharedCollocates.values().stream().mapToDouble(Double::doubleValue).sum(); double avg = count == 0 ? 0.0 : sum / count;

findings.summary: identifyCoreCollocates sorts with a manual two-level lambda rather than Comparator.comparingInt(...).thenComparingDouble(...)
evidence: coreCollocates.sort((a, b) -> { int cmp = Integer.compare(b.sharedByCount(), a.sharedByCount()); return cmp != 0 ? cmp : Double.compare(b.avgLogDice(), a.avgLogDice()); })
Standard Java Comparator composition (comparingInt.reversed().thenComparingDouble.reversed()) is more readable and consistent
findings.suggestion: Use Comparator.comparingInt(CoreCollocate::sharedByCount).reversed().thenComparingDouble(CoreCollocate::avgLogDice).reversed() to match standard Java comparator composition style.

findings.summary: CollocateProfileComparator logs method inputs and per-item object dumps at debug level, a hallmark AI entry-logging pattern
evidence: logger.debug("Nouns: {}", seedNouns) and logger.debug("Min logDice: {}", minLogDice) at top of compareCollocateProfiles — logs inputs visible at the call site
logger.debug("Profiling {}: {} adjectives (top: {})", noun, adjectives.size(), adjectives.subList(0, Math.min(5, adjectives.size()))) inside per-noun loop — object dump at debug level
debugLogTopProfiles iterates profiles emitting per-item debug lines — full object dump loop
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one meaningful progress log per method call. Remove per-item object dumps from inside loops; move to trace level if needed for diagnostics.

findings.summary: SingleSeedExplorationOptions delegation accessors have one-line Javadocs that restate the accessor name without adding insight
evidence: /** @return maximum collocates to retrieve per seed. */ public int topCollocates() { return base.topCollocates(); }
/** @return minimum logDice threshold for collocate inclusion. */ public double logDiceThreshold() { return base.logDiceThreshold(); }
Each Javadoc says nothing a developer cannot infer from the method name; delegation is already apparent from the body
findings.suggestion: Remove the Javadocs on these three delegation accessors, or replace with a single class-level note that they delegate to base() for call-site convenience.

findings.summary: Three assertTrue(list.size() >= 0) assertions in ExploreResponseAssemblerTest are always true and test nothing
evidence: assertTrue(response.seedCollocates().size() >= 0, "should have seed_collocates_count") — List.size() is always >= 0
assertTrue(response.primarySeeds().size() >= 0, "should have discovered_nouns_count") — same tautology
assertTrue(response.coreCollocates().size() >= 0, "should have core_collocates_count") — same tautology
The fixture provides 1 seedCollocate, 1 discoveredNoun, 1 coreCollocate — asserting specific counts would catch real regressions
findings.suggestion: Replace with assertEquals(1, response.seedCollocates().size()), assertEquals(1, response.primarySeeds().size()), assertEquals(1, response.coreCollocates().size()) matching the provided fixture values.

findings.summary: assertEquals(2, response.seedCollocates().size()) appears twice consecutively in populateExploreResponse_countsMatchListSizes
evidence: Line 104: assertEquals(2, response.seedCollocates().size());
Line 105: assertEquals(2, response.seedCollocates().size()); // exact duplicate
The second assertion was likely meant to check a different property (e.g. seedCollocatesCount if the record exposed one) but instead repeats the same check
findings.suggestion: Remove the duplicate assertion. If a count companion field exists on the response, assert it separately: assertEquals(response.seedCollocates().size(), response.seedCollocatesCount()).

findings.summary: query/ mixes interface definitions with BlackLab-specific implementation classes in one flat package
evidence: query/ contains 3 port interfaces (QueryExecutor, CollocateQueryPort, SketchQueryPort) alongside 3 BlackLab-specific concrete classes (BlackLabQueryExecutor, CollocateQueryHelper, BlackLabSnippetParser) in the same flat package

The project deliberately created exploration/spi/ as a dedicated sub-package to isolate the ExplorationService interface from its SemanticFieldExplorer implementation — that same port/adapter separation principle is not applied in query/
A developer navigating to query/ to 'find the query interface' sees implementation detail mixed with contract definitions without any structural cue to distinguish them
findings.suggestion: Create a query/blacklab/ sub-package and move BlackLabQueryExecutor, CollocateQueryHelper, and BlackLabSnippetParser into it, keeping query/ for port interfaces only — mirroring the exploration/spi/ pattern already used elsewhere in the project.

findings.summary: model.PosGroup is the only non-package-info class at model/ root while all peers live in model/exploration/ or model/sketch/
evidence: model/ root contains only PosGroup.java and package-info.java; all other model types live in model/exploration/ (10 files) or model/sketch/ (5 files)
PosGroup is imported by both exploration and sketch layer classes, making it a shared vocabulary type with no obvious home in either sub-package
A developer browsing model/ would expect either a flat list of types or consistently sub-organized types — not a single outlier at root with two deep sub-packages alongside it
findings.suggestion: Either move PosGroup into a model/common/ or model/shared/ sub-package that explicitly groups cross-cutting vocabulary types, or add a comment in model/package-info.java explaining that root-level types are shared across both sub-packages to set developer expectations.

findings.summary: WordSketchApiServer security model Javadoc omits the wildcard CORS opt-in mechanism that effectively expands the trust boundary
evidence: WordSketchApiServer class Javadoc documents the loopback-only security model: 'All endpoints are unauthenticated — access control is provided by the host firewall and the loopback-only bind'

HttpApiUtils.warnIfWildcardCors() allows CORS origin to be set to '*' via -Dallow.wildcard.cors=true, which permits cross-origin requests from any browser origin and materially expands the effective trust surface beyond loopback

The security model Javadoc in WordSketchApiServer says 'Any change to the bind address must be accompanied by an authentication layer' but does not mention that CORS origin expansion via system property also alters the trust boundary without changing the bind address
findings.suggestion: Add a sentence to the WordSketchApiServer security model Javadoc paragraph noting that CORS origin is configurable via -Dcors.allow.origin=<origin> and that the wildcard opt-in (-Dallow.wildcard.cors=true) changes the effective trust surface; cross-reference HttpApiUtils.warnIfWildcardCors().

findings.summary: jackson-core is explicitly pinned to work around a transitive version conflict, creating fragile dependency management
evidence: pom.xml explicitly declares jackson-core 2.17.2 with the comment 'Pin jackson-core to match databind; another transitive dep resolves an older version otherwise'

The jackson-bom is already imported in dependencyManagement to manage Jackson versions consistently, yet jackson-core still requires an explicit pin — indicating something in the transitive tree (likely BlackLab or a BlackLab transitive dependency) overrides the BOM-managed version
Without a Maven lock file, a future BlackLab update could silently re-introduce the conflict or make the pin diverge; the conflicting dependency is not identified by name in the comment
findings.suggestion: Run 'mvn dependency:tree -Dincludes=com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-core' to identify the dependency pulling in the older version, and update the pom.xml comment to name it explicitly. Verify whether the jackson-bom exclusion or a <dependencyManagement> override on that specific transitive dep can replace the direct pin.

findings.summary: blacklab-github-fallback Maven profile registers a raw.githubusercontent.com URL as a repository with no SLA
evidence: The blacklab-github-fallback profile registers https://raw.githubusercontent.com/instituutnederlandsetaal/BlackLab/mvn-repo/ as a Maven repository
The pom.xml comment itself warns 'The URL has no SLA and may become unavailable (rate-limited, branch deleted, or repo renamed); do NOT rely on it in production CI'
Despite the warning, the profile remains in pom.xml and could be activated by passing -P blacklab-github-fallback in any CI pipeline without further review
findings.suggestion: Remove the blacklab-github-fallback profile from pom.xml entirely and document only the install-blacklab.sh local-install path as the supported setup. If a remote fallback is genuinely needed, mirror the JAR to a private registry (Nexus, Artifactory, or GitHub Packages) and update the profile to point there instead.

findings.summary: The 'migrate to Undertow/Jetty' architectural decision is tracked as a TODO in two separate files, creating ambiguity about which is authoritative
evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java:55 contains: '// TODO: migrate to Undertow or Jetty if a multi-module build or custom classloader is needed.'
pom.xml maven-compiler-plugin comment also reads: 'TODO: consider migrating to Undertow or Jetty if a multi-module build, custom classloader environment, or EE-style deployment is ever needed.'
Both TODOs describe the same conditional architectural decision with slightly different wording; a developer searching for migration context must check both places
findings.suggestion: Remove the TODO from pom.xml (the build descriptor is not the natural home for a runtime architectural decision) and keep the single authoritative note in WordSketchApiServer.java where it is closest to the affected code. Alternatively, consolidate to an ARCHITECTURE.md decision record and remove both inline TODOs.

findings.summary: WordSketchApiServer constructor calls RelationUtils.validateAliases() — a startup side effect — alongside pure dependency wiring
evidence: The constructor comment reads 'Port binding is deferred to start() so that construction and network binding are separated', establishing a clear design principle: construction = pure wiring, start() = side effects

Despite this stated principle, the constructor calls RelationUtils.validateAliases(grammarConfig) which logs warnings and reads from grammarConfig — a side-effectful startup validation, not pure wiring
A test that constructs WordSketchApiServer but never calls start() will still trigger the alias-validation logging side effect, violating the construction/startup separation principle
findings.suggestion: Move the RelationUtils.validateAliases(grammarConfig) call from the constructor into the start() method, alongside HttpApiUtils.warnIfWildcardCors(), keeping the constructor as pure field assignment and handler construction.

findings.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer 4-arg constructor accepts @Nullable GrammarConfig as a test escape hatch, adding conditional branching to the primary production path
evidence: The 4-arg constructor is documented as the 'Primary constructor' and 'Use this in production wiring code' but accepts @Nullable GrammarConfig with a test-only null escape hatch
When grammarConfig is null the constructor silently falls back to the hardcoded FALLBACK_NOUN_PATTERN; a production caller passing null by mistake gets no exception, only degraded behavior
The 2-arg convenience constructor is actually the safer production path (it cannot accept null grammarConfig from a Java caller), yet the Javadoc directs production callers to the nullable 4-arg form
findings.suggestion: Remove the @Nullable annotation and null-branch from the 4-arg constructor (make grammarConfig @NonNull); for tests that need to bypass grammar config, add a package-private or @VisibleForTesting static factory method SemanticFieldExplorer.withFallbackPattern(executor, comparator, multiSeedExplorer) that sets the hardcoded pattern explicitly, making the test intent visible without contaminating the production API.

findings.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser has residual null-return strategy mixed with throw strategy, bounded within the package but inconsistent
evidence: Some parsing methods return null while others throw IllegalArgumentException for the same class of parse failure
findings.suggestion: Complete the migration: replace remaining null returns with IllegalArgumentException to fully align with the throw strategy

findings.summary: CORS allowed origin is read from System.getProperty per-request rather than captured at startup in WordSketchApiServer.start()
evidence: CORS origin property read inside sendResponse() on every request instead of being resolved once in start()
findings.suggestion: Capture CORS origin string in WordSketchApiServer.start() and pass to HttpApiUtils, consistent with how port and other server config is handled
